text: Video-LLaMA: Exploiting Visual and Audio Cues with Large Language Models We introduce Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework that leverages both the visual and audio cues present in a video to assist Large Language Models (LLMs) in grounding their outputs to the actual content shown in a video. Video-LLaMA starts by bootstrapping cross-modal training from the frozen pre-trained visual and audio features and the frozen LLMs. The main contribution of Video-LLaMA is to address the two major hurdles in grounding LLMs to videos: (1) modelling temporal visual structures and (2) integrating audio-visual modalities. To address the first hurdle, the Video Q-former is proposed to transform a pre-trained image encoder into a video encoder. The video-to-text generation task is introduced to learn the video-language correspondences. To address the second hurdle, the ImageBind (IB) is adopted as the pre-trained audio encoder. The Audio Q-former is further proposed to learn reasonable audio query embeddings for the LLM module. To match the visual/audio features to the embedding space of the LLM, we pre-train Video-LLaMA with the massive video/image-caption pairs and fine-tune Video-LLaMA with moderate amounts of visual-instruction datasets with higher quality. Experimental results demonstrate the capacity of Video-LLaMA in sensing the video content and generating output responses that are grounded on the visual and audio cues within the videos.
explanation: The text is coherent, uses authentic-looking academic structure, and introduces coined terms like Video Q-former and Audio Q-former, which can be characteristic of AI-generated abstracts. Still, it could be genuine scientific writing as well, so the probability is moderate.
